name: case_4_multiple_bridging_sentences
description: Between 1a and 1b, a typical long-form CAIE setup has 2 bridging sentences — a variable definition + a geometric condition. VL swallowed BOTH into 1a.qt. 1b.ps correctly accumulated both. Rescue greedy-strips longest match first: tries last 3 sentences → no match, tries last 2 → MATCH (both sentences appear in 1b.ps), strips both. Final 1a.qt contains only the real instruction.

pre_items.parent_stem: A continuous random variable X has probability density function f(x) = kx^2 for 0 <= x <= 2 and zero elsewhere.
pre_items.question_text: Find the value of the constant k. Given that Y = 3X + 1, it follows that E(Y) = 3E(X) + 1. It is also given that Var(Y) = 9 Var(X).

pre_items.parent_stem: A continuous random variable X has probability density function f(x) = kx^2 for 0 <= x <= 2 and zero elsewhere. Given that Y = 3X + 1, it follows that E(Y) = 3E(X) + 1. It is also given that Var(Y) = 9 Var(X).

expected_strip_log: 1a: stripped trailing 2 sentences 'Given that Y = 3X + 1, it follows that E(Y) = 3E(X) + 1. It is also given that Var(Y) = 9 Var(X).' (matched in 1b.parent_stem)
